lines.line: > Tara Leach provides insight into how Covid-19 first impacted her professionally as fourth grade teacher. She explains what technology she used to teach when students were sent home to learn. Leach describes technological challenges, boredom with students, and dropping supplies on student's homes. Additionally, Leach shares how she embraced learning during the pandemic so she could better teach her students in a remote and eventually hybrid setting.
**Covid-19, Google Slides, Google Classroom, Google Meets, Bitmoji, Learning, Educators, Boise School District**

metadata.title: Covid-19 Experiences
metadata.synopsis: Tara Leach provides insight into how Covid-19 first impacted her professionally as fourth grade teacher. She explains what technology she used to teach when students were sent home to learn. Leach describes technological challenges, boredom with students, and dropping supplies on student's homes. Additionally, Leach shares how she embraced learning during the pandemic so she could better teach her students in a remote and eventually hybrid setting.
metadata.keywords: Covid-19, Google Slides, Google Classroom, Google Meets, Bitmoji, Learning, Educators, Boise School District

lines.line: # Masking and Teaching During the Pandemic

> Leach explains what it was like to teach while students, staff, and teachers were masked. She noted that students remained socially distanced and stayed in pods of four when they went to other areas, such as the cafeteria and physical education. She also noted that she started doing teaching activities outside so that her students could take mask breaks. Lastly, Leach notes that in order to assist with catching students up who were behind due to the pandemic/remote learning, the Boise School District implemented a "power up" period to focus on specific subjects.
**Boise School District, pods, social distancing, math, writing, reading, curriculum,**
keywords: **Boise School District, pods, social distancing, math, writing, reading, curriculum,**

metadata.title: Masking and Teaching During the Pandemic
metadata.synopsis: Leach explains what it was like to teach while students, staff, and teachers were masked. She noted that students remained socially distanced and stayed in pods of four when they went to other areas, such as the cafeteria and physical education. She also noted that she started doing teaching activities outside so that her students could take mask breaks. Lastly, Leach notes that in order to assist with catching students up who were behind due to the pandemic/remote learning, the Boise School District implemented a "power up" period to focus on specific subjects.
metadata.keywords: Boise School District, pods, social distancing, math, writing, reading, curriculum,

lines.line: > Tara Leach describes her interactions with parents during the pandemic. She noted the politics associated with masks and how it was particularly challenging for young students. Leach also shares how isolating it could feel teaching in a vacuum and not being able to interact with fellow teachers, staff, or administrators. She noted that many teachers quit during the pandemic because it was so stressful and difficult.

lines.line: > Tara Leach described the challenges of parenting two daughters during the pandemic. She noted that during the pandemic the family would cook, hike, ride bikes, and finish puzzles together. She shared that friends would meet in parking lots and stay 6 feet part so that they could catch up and visit.

metadata.title: Wrap Up
metadata.synopsis: Tara Leach concluded her oral history interview by sharing that she felt like people in her community tried really hard to come together and support one another during the pandemic. She also noted that she wanted future generations to know that public school teachers did everything they could to continue to teach and support students throughout the pandemic.

children.text: > Interviewer Stephanie Milne-Lane and Narrator Tara Leach conduct introductions. Leach provides background on her time in Idaho.
**Boise School District, teacher, Riverside Elementary, East Boise,**

> Tara Leach provides insight into how Covid-19 first impacted her professionally as fourth grade teacher. She explains what technology she used to teach when students were sent home to learn. Leach describes technological challenges, boredom with students, and dropping supplies on student's homes. Additionally, Leach shares how she embraced learning during the pandemic so she could better teach her students in a remote and eventually hybrid setting.
**Covid-19, Google Slides, Google Classroom, Google Meets, Bitmoji, Learning, Educators, Boise School District**
# Masking and Teaching During the Pandemic

> Leach explains what it was like to teach while students, staff, and teachers were masked. She noted that students remained socially distanced and stayed in pods of four when they went to other areas, such as the cafeteria and physical education. She also noted that she started doing teaching activities outside so that her students could take mask breaks. Lastly, Leach notes that in order to assist with catching students up who were behind due to the pandemic/remote learning, the Boise School District implemented a "power up" period to focus on specific subjects.
**Boise School District, pods, social distancing, math, writing, reading, curriculum,**

> Tara Leach describes her interactions with parents during the pandemic. She noted the politics associated with masks and how it was particularly challenging for young students. Leach also shares how isolating it could feel teaching in a vacuum and not being able to interact with fellow teachers, staff, or administrators. She noted that many teachers quit during the pandemic because it was so stressful and difficult.

> Tara Leach described the challenges of parenting two daughters during the pandemic. She noted that during the pandemic the family would cook, hike, ride bikes, and finish puzzles together. She shared that friends would meet in parking lots and stay 6 feet part so that they could catch up and visit.
**parenting, sports, activities, hiking, Boise Foothills, puzzles,**

> Tara Leach concluded her oral history interview by sharing that she felt like people in her community tried really hard to come together and support one another during the pandemic. She also noted that she wanted future generations to know that public school teachers did everything they could to continue to teach and support students throughout the pandemic.

story.description: Tara shares her experiences as a teacher and a mother.
